steps.title: Identify the slope
steps.content: $F$ is linear in $x$ with slope $\tfrac{9}{5}$, so each $1$-kelvin increase raises $F$ by $\tfrac{9}{5}$ degrees.

steps.title: Use the source rationale
steps.content: Choice A is correct. It's given that the function $F\left(x\right)=\frac{9}{5}\left(x-273.15\right)+32$ gives the temperature, in degrees Fahrenheit, that corresponds to a temperature of $x$ kelvins. A temperature that increased by $9.10$ kelvins means that the value of $x$ increased by $9.10$ kelvins. It follows that an increase in $x$ by $9.10$ increases $F(x)$ by $\frac{9}{5}\left(9.10\right)$, or $16.38$. Therefore, if a temperature increased by $9.10$ kelvins, the temperature increased by $16.38$ degrees Fahrenheit.

steps.title: Check the choices
steps.content: Choice B is incorrect and may result from conceptual or calculation errors.
